articles.headline: 'Finally': Norway star Erling Haaland on fulfilling World Cup dream
articles.description: Erling Haaland tells ESPN he is ready to make his World Cup debut and show his love for Norway on football's biggest stage.

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.caption: Erling Haaland has 55 goals in 50 games for Norway.
images.credit: Jared C. Tilton - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Jordan Henderson: Luka Modric is the best midfielder I've faced
articles.description: Jordan Henderson has praised Luka Modric as the greatest midfielder he has ever faced, ahead of England's match against Croatia on Wednesday.

images.name: Jordan Henderson Luka Modric [1296x729]
images.alt: Jordan Henderson challenges Luka Modric in the 2018 World Cup semifinal.

images.name: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener
images.caption: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: New Zealand's match-predicting puppy poops on live TV
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Oli Sail, Tova O'Brien and Chris Chang with Bear the dog on TVNZ's Breakfast [1080x608]
Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany
images.caption: Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany

articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Belgium's Thibaut Courtois World Cup 2026 portrait [1296x729]
images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

images.name: Rod Stewart [1296x729]
images.alt: Rod Stewart celebrates at the game between Scotland and Haiti.
images.credit: (Photo by Martin Rickett/PA Images via Getty Images)

images.name: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston
images.caption: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston

articles.headline: Explained: How a chip in the ball allowed World Cup's fastest-ever goal by a sub to stand
articles.description: VAR overturned an offside decision after ruling there was a slight flick on the ball by Sweden's Alexander Isak, thanks to some technology in the ball.

images.name: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal [1296x729]
images.caption: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal
images.credit: Manuel Velasquez - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win
images.caption: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win

article.headline: France finish top as Ecuador exit
article.linkText: Ecuador 0-0 France: Les Bleus top group

images.name: Paul Pogba (France) Shot Off Target at 73'
images.caption: Paul Pogba (France) Shot Off Target at 73'

article.story:
France secured top spot in Group E and a last 16 showdown with Nigeria as Ecuador's Antonio Valencia saw red for a two-footed lunge in the Maracana.

- Marcotti: Deschamps manages French expectations
- Delaney: Improvement needed for France

Manchester United midfielder Valencia, also sent off for tangling with Raheem Sterling in the friendly against England in Miami earlier this month, went over the top of the ball on Lucas Digne, leaving the referee in no doubt.

Ecuador fought on with 10 men to the end but, needing to win by two goals as a result of Switzerland's easy win over Honduras, had only pride as their reward for the 0-0 draw.

The game may have developed differently had the match officials spotted Mamadou Sakho's nasty elbow on Oswaldo Minda early on in the game.

Had Ivory Coast referee Noumandiez Doue seen the incident there is little doubt that Sakho would have been off, and it may be that he faces retrospective action from FIFA.

This was a tightly-fought affair from the start, with France shading the first half and Ecuador content to play on the break.

There were a series of half-chances for France -- Antoine Griezmann was a yard wide with a low strike, then Moussa Sissoko had a swing with a left-footed volley which was too easy for Ecuador goalkeeper Alexander Dominguez.

The best chance came when Digne, Paris Saint-Germain's impressive 20-year-old left-back who was also making his World Cup debut, won a free kick. Griezmann floated it over for Paul Pogba to thump a header goalwards but Dominguez was able to tip it over.

After Ecuador's Christian Noboa was forced to don a hair-net to cover up a bloody wound incurred from a clash of heads with Blaise Matuidi, the South Americans began to improve.

Michael Arroyo made a surging run and Enner Valencia was waiting to pounce but the midfielder tried to cross with the outside of his foot and it was too close to Hugo Lloris.

Enner Valencia, easily Ecuador's player of the tournament, did win a good header but once again Lloris was equal to it.

Dominguez then denied Griezmann but he knew little about it. Sagna's cross was met by Griezmann's powerful header which bobbled off the keeper's hands and on to the post before being cleared.

Then came Valencia's lunge on Digne, raking his studs down the full-back's leg and had it not been for a shinpad the outcome could have been much worse. France coach Didier Deschamps looked concerned on the touchline but after treatment Digne was able to continue.

Ecuador nearly caught France napping with a terrific break by Enner Valencia and Noboa should have scored but his first touch was horrendous and he sliced his shot well wide.

Matuidi was next to threaten after a nice touch from Karim Benzema but Dominguez saved well again, before Pogba was horribly wasteful from a free header at the far post.

Benzema failed to trouble Dominguez from 20 yards, then after a quick break Michael Arroyo shot wildly wide for Ecuador.

Ecuador continued to threaten on the counter-attack, and Alex Ibarra had a powerful strike beaten away by Lloris, then there were more heroics from man-of-the-match Dominguez to keep out Benzema, Loic Remy and Olivier Giroud.

It may have been a comedown for France after their high-scoring exploits against Switzerland and Honduras but Deschamps will not complain -- Les Bleus are unbeaten and marching into the last 16.
